title: Jace Marquardt
text:
About Me

Ocean Glider Lab studies how coastal circulation, water properties, and climate variability shape ocean conditions along the shelf. The work combines autonomous underwater vehicles, gliders, ship-based measurements, and data products that make long observing records easier to use.
This page uses the same profile-style structure as the reference site: a clear name block, a short research summary, and stacked sections for credentials, experience, and links.

title: Jace Marquardt
text:
Research



Autonomous observing


Glider deployments, sensor systems, and repeat transects that capture changing coastal conditions over time.




Coastal processes


Currents, upwelling, oxygen variability, and the physical drivers that connect the shelf to marine ecosystems.




Data products


Analysis, visualization, and well-documented datasets that support students, collaborators, and field teams.

title: Jace Marquardt
text:
Education



Graduate training in oceanography, marine science, or a related coastal observing field.


Undergraduate foundation in the physical sciences, mathematics, engineering, or environmental studies.


Ongoing learning through field deployments, coding, mentoring, and collaboration.

title: Jace Marquardt
text:
Experience



Research and field work on underwater gliders, moorings, and coastal observing projects.


Lab and teaching support for students working with ocean data, instrumentation, and scientific communication.


Project coordination across observing campaigns, data management, and collaborative research efforts.
